price_details: Their site lists photo packages plus aerial video, aerial photography, and Matterports as add-ons, but says an actual price brochure is coming soon, so nothing is posted yet.
notes: Katie is the photographer behind Studio Kiva and she also offers Matterport and aerial video for anyone who wants drone shots of the venue. All 13 reviews on Google are five stars, several calling out her warmth and how relaxed she makes the process. One reviewer specifically said her pricing felt fair for the quality, but you will have to reach out directly since nothing is posted online yet.

price_details: Elopement coverage starts with an average investment around $5,800. Destination pricing is tiered by location: anywhere in the continental US outside Colorado starts at $5,500, Hawaii and Alaska start at $6,000, Mexico and Central America start at $6,000, Europe starts at $7,000, and Africa, Australia, and Southeast Asia start at $8,000. Engagement sessions are offered separately and packages include an option to add one in.
notes: Tayler is based out of Evergreen but her site is built around destination elopements, with a full price ladder by continent. Reviews keep mentioning that her packages include an engagement session as a way to get comfortable in front of the camera before the wedding, and one bride said she went into the wedding day already knowing the photos would turn out well. Another review specifically praised how she captures movement and color and how at ease she makes nervous subjects feel.

price_details: Her site has a pricing and info page but it only lists that she helps with elopement planning and guidance, no actual numbers, so you'd need to email her directly for rates.
notes: Taylor specializes in intimate weddings and elopements and her site says she loves when they end with dancing under the stars. Reviews go back several years but are detailed: one bride said she shot an engagement-style session in Sedona and the wedding itself in Keystone, another said she traveled to Iceland and spent 12 hours with the couple documenting the day. She clearly does destination work well beyond Colorado, she just doesn't post pricing anywhere.

price_details: Site says to reach out directly to book a session and doesn't list any package pricing for weddings or elopements.
notes: Based in Edwards, they cover engagements, elopements, and weddings, and packages include both a photographer and videographer together according to the site. Reviews are mostly about family and anniversary sessions rather than actual weddings, with people praising Chris for working quickly with kids and bringing along drone equipment. Sounds solid for photo plus video coverage but you're inquiring blind on cost since nothing is published.

sources: Rebecca is the photographer behind Third Eye, based in Crested Butte, and her site specifically says she avoids the contrived Pinterest pose thing in favor of documenting things as they happen. She covers weddings, engagements, and elopements and says she'll travel anywhere if your wedding is out of Colorado, one review even mentions she shot a wedding out in Charlottesville, VA. Reviews are a mix of wedding and family session feedback, several say she makes people feel at ease and barely noticeable while shooting.

notes: Three inclusive packages starting at $5,000, with custom quotes available if your coverage doesn't fit the standard hourly tiers. A phone call is offered before booking if you want to talk it through first. She shoots both film and digital and specializes in Scotland and Ireland elopements specifically, though she says she'll travel wherever, and she also thrifts vintage photo albums and handwrites love letters for her couples as a keepsake.
photos: Mandie runs Thistle and Pine out of Colorado Springs and her site leans hard into a nostalgic, film-forward style, one review called it capturing things in an authentic yet elevated way rather than a staged version of the day. A couple from Massachusetts said their communication with her from Colorado was excellent even before they met in person for the actual shoot. Multiple reviews specifically praised her humor and how she made posing feel less intimidating for camera-shy people.

notes: Collections start at $5,850 with your date locked in via signed contract and a retainer. The site notes separate permit fees for certain shoot locations, $50 for one and $300 for another, though it doesn't say which spots those apply to. She's based in Colorado Springs but says she's available worldwide for destination weddings and elopements.
photos: One review said choosing Tina was the single most agonized-over decision of the whole wedding, more than the dress or the venue, and it paid off. Another mentions Summer, who appears to be a second shooter on her team, traveling offsite with the couple for mountain photos and even helping bustle the bride's dress. A different reviewer said Tina handled a wedding-day hiccup professionally, which is a good sign for how she reacts when something actually goes wrong.

price_details: Site quotes wedding photography in the Beaver Creek and Boulder markets typically running $2,500 to $9,000. The specific package listed is 7 hours of coverage for $3,450, with fully edited images delivered within 72 hours, high resolution and print ready. He also has dedicated pricing pages for Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat Springs, and Vail, so this looks like a mountain-town-focused operation with Fort Collins as his base.
notes: Tom covers a wide swath of Colorado ski towns and one review said his images balance traditional posed shots with more candid emotional moments. A standout story: one couple's original photographer's business closed two months before their wedding, and they said Tom stepped in, was warm and responsive from the first call, and put them at ease during what could have been a stressful scramble. Another review mentioned he calls ahead of the wedding to walk through the day's timeline, which is a nice touch if you don't have a planner.
sources: site pricing pages; google reviews; launchintel digest
